intro: Your <1>datasets</1> are the focus. Each dataset has a "<3>design (its words)</3>" and "<5>ingest rules</5>".

focusBanner.label: Kind of data behind this Ask citation:
focusBanner.sub: This answer is based on data of this kind.

empty.title: No datasets yet
empty.sub: Ingest data via "Add data" and it will appear here.

sharedBand.title: Shared terms
sharedBand.warn: Changes affect everything · handle with care
sharedBand.sub: The schema multiple datasets share. Keeping it aligned lets you <1>search and compare across datasets</1>.

crosswalkBand.title: Connections
crosswalkBand.sub: Connect datasets that share the same concept (composition, crystal system, author…) with <1>a single bridge</1>. It can answer "how many datasets report this value?" across datasets.

tools.none: This dataset has no answerable tools yet because it has not been imported. Run "Import data" first.

files.head: Ingested files
files.dedupe: Every file that makes up this dataset. Files with the same name are detected and flagged at ingest time.
files.futureDedup: * Content-hash duplicate detection is coming; for now you can check by file name.
files.none: No ingested-file records yet. Use the operations below to ingest.

connect.head: This dataset's connections
connect.none: This dataset is not yet connected to other data.
connect.createBtn: Connect this to your other data

mapBand.title: Overview
mapBand.sub: See <1>what data exists and how it connects</1> in a single map.

design.title: Schema (structure of the content)
design.predicatesHead: Fields in use (from the actual data)
design.reusesHead: Standard words borrowed from elsewhere (detected from the actual data)
design.advisoryNote: Use “review the design” below to have the AI add the links.

rules.title: Ingest rules (artifacts)
rules.projectionIntro: A plain-language reading generated from the stored ingest rules — a deterministic transformation, no AI involved. The exact executable original (RML form) is always available in the artifact files below.
rules.subject: How the ID is built

kind.unknown: This form has no plain-language reading yet (check the raw file)

rules.legacyNote: Not executed by the declarative ingest path (legacy artifact).
rules.loadError: Failed to load the ingest rules: {{message}}

history.head: Change history (redesigns)
history.diffIntro: Comparison between the version replaced on {{when}} and the current one (− old, ＋ current).
history.noChanges: No differences between this version and the current one.
history.unchangedNote: Files without differences are not shown.

ingest.head: Import data
ingest.noRml: This design has no ingest rules yet, so it cannot be ingested. Create them via "Add data" (the AI designs them), and you will be able to load safely from here.
ingest.done: ✓ Loaded into the unpublished draft ({{n}} facts). Next, press "Publish for search" below so Ask can cite it.
ingest.note: On approval, the ingest rules run and the result lands in an <1>unpublished draft</1> (visible to no one until you publish). Only safety-vetted transformations run — no AI-written code is executed.
ingest.noteDocument: On approval, the saved document is structured into sections → paragraphs → sentences and loaded into the unpublished draft (visible to no one until you publish). Published data is untouched.
ingest.sourceSaved: The design-time {{source}} is saved{{files}}. Ingest with no re-attach.
ingest.pickPlaceholder: Choose the {{source}} used for the design
ingest.validationHead: Validating the ingest rules before the run found these problems (fix them and try again):
ingest.cancelled: Ingest cancelled. Any partially written data has been discarded — nothing reached the published surface.

append.head: Append to live (device feed)
append.note: Ingest only the new batch {{source}} and append it to the published data as-is (only the new part is processed, so it is fast even for large data). Existing facts and IDs are unchanged and identical rows never duplicate. The design and first version were approved at publish time, so appends skip the gate (for continuous device logs). Match the filename to the source filename fixed at design time (the design's source field).
append.done: ✓ Appended (+{{n}} triples · batch #{{seq}}). Ask can cite it immediately.

promote.ok: ✓ Published for search ({{n}} facts{{version}}). Ask can cite it with its source.
promote.repromoteNote: A new version is loaded into the unpublished draft. Pressing "Update publication (re-publish)" bumps the version Ask cites v{{version}} → v{{next}} and switches to the new data. The switch is instant and the old version is cleaned up automatically (the current version keeps being cited during ingest, so there is no gap).
promote.note: Publishing for search turns this unpublished draft into the <1>published data Ask cites</1>. Before publishing, you can check which of its words reuse the standard ones and which are new to this dataset.
promote.alignmentSummary: Fields: {{predReuse}} standard as-is · {{predNew}} new / Kinds: {{classReuse}} standard as-is · {{classNew}} new
promote.alignmentNew: Field words new to this dataset: {{terms}}
promote.submit: Publish for search (make usable in Ask)

reingest.head: Update data (re-ingest)
reingest.notePrefix: Ingest the new {{source}} as a separate draft version (the published data stays as-is).
reingest.notePublished: The current published version (v{{version}}) keeps being cited by Ask during ingest, so answers are not interrupted. When done, "Update publication (re-publish)" below switches v{{version}} → v{{next}} and the old version is cleaned up automatically.
reingest.noteUnpublished: Re-ingests an unpublished draft (no effect on Ask). When done, you can publish it via "Publish for search" below.
reingest.sourceSaved: The design-time {{source}} is saved{{files}}. Ingest with no re-attach. To swap in a different {{source}}, select it below.
reingest.placeholderKeep: If you do not replace it, the saved {{source}} is used

redesign.head: Revise the design
redesign.note: Reopen this schema and ingest rules in the workbench to fix or adjust them, then save (it updates the same dataset — no need to delete and recreate). After saving, apply the data via "Update data (re-ingest)".
redesign.noProposal: This dataset has no stored design to reopen in the workbench (it was created before the design was persisted). Please recreate it instead.

lifecycle.head: Lifecycle actions
lifecycle.retractedStatus: Status: <1>Retracted</1> (excluded from Ask citations. The data and IDs remain, so existing citations are not broken. You can reinstate it.)
lifecycle.retractConfirm:
Retract this dataset?
It will be removed from Ask citations, but the data and IDs remain, so existing citations are not broken (you can reinstate it later).
lifecycle.retractDone: Retracted (excluded from the published data · existing citations are not broken).
lifecycle.deleteConfirmPromoted:
Permanently delete this dataset?
It is published (citable), so existing citations may return 404. "Retract" is usually recommended.

Delete anyway?
lifecycle.deleteConfirm: Delete this dataset? (Not published, so it is safe to delete.)

docAppend.head: Add a document (to this dataset)
docAppend.note: Add one more Word <0>.docx</0> / structured XML <1>.xml</1> / PDF, and it is structured into sections → paragraphs → sentences and <2>appended directly to the shared data (live)</2>. From then on, <3>search_text</3> / <4>quote_with_citation</4> in the "Tools" tab <5>search and cite the added document too</5> (for accumulating meeting minutes). <6>.docx</6> and PDF are auto-converted to XML server-side (the converter and version are recorded in provenance).
docAppend.done: ✓ Document added (+{{n}} facts). You can search and cite the full text from the "Tools" tab.
docAppend.doneN: ✓ Added {{docs}} document(s) (+{{n}} facts). You can search and cite the full text from the "Tools" tab.

api.ontologyDescription: A design materialized in the workbench ({{date}}). A draft before promotion into the shared vocabulary.

rmlSummary.promoted: Ingest rules (loaded into the published data · {{n}} facts)
rmlSummary.ingested: Ingest rules (loaded into the unpublished draft · {{n}} facts)
rmlSummary.design: Ingest rules (not ingested yet. You can ingest safely from this screen.)

mappingDescription.promoted: In the published data (what Ask cites) · {{n}} facts. Also queryable via Advanced search (SPARQL).
mappingDescription.ingested: In the unpublished draft · {{n}} facts. Once verified, "Publish for search" makes it citable by Ask.
mappingDescription.design: Design only — no data ingested yet. With ingest rules present, you can ingest safely from this screen.

api.mappingIrSummary: The reviewed ingest-rule spec (the executable rules are generated from it deterministically)

advisory.disconnectedPair: “{{a}}” and “{{b}}” are not linked. They are recorded as separate things, so questions that span both — which {{a}} belongs to this {{b}} — cannot be answered.
advisory.disconnected: {{count}} groups of data are not linked to each other, so questions spanning them cannot be answered.
advisory.duplicateColumn_one: {{count}} column is written twice, onto two different things. Choosing one owner removes the duplicated facts.
advisory.duplicateColumn_other: {{count}} columns are written twice, onto two different things. Choosing one owner each removes the duplicated facts.
advisory.unmapped_one: {{count}} column is not used. If it carries meaning, adding it to the design makes it questionable.
advisory.unmapped_other: {{count}} columns are not used. If they carry meaning, adding them to the design makes them questionable.
